item.title: Hong Kong overtakes the US as the second favorite destination for Chinese students
item.description:
Hong Kong has overtaken the United States as the second favorite destination for Chinese students planning to study abroad. The United Kingdom remains the main destination for the seventh consecutive year, while Hong Kong's rise is attributed to the quality of higher education, geographic proximity and talent attraction policies.
Hong Kong has surpassed the United States for the first time as the second preferred destination for Chinese students planning to study abroad, according to a survey by the New Oriental educational institution. The report, released by the Beijing News newspaper, analyzes the evolution of students' profiles, chosen destinations and decision criteria, pointing to increasing diversification and a more “pragmatic” approach on the part of families. The United Kingdom remains the main destination for the seventh consecutive year, while Hong Kong's rise is attributed to the quality of higher education, geographic proximity and talent attraction policies. On the other hand, the decline of the United States coincides with the worsening of bilateral relations, with political, commercial and technological tensions also being reflected in the education sector. About a year ago, Washington toughened its position in relation to Chinese students, with greater scrutiny on granting visas, restrictions in areas considered sensitive and accusations of links to the Chinese Communist Party, measures that Beijing considers “discriminatory” and a “politicization of cooperation in the area of ​​education”. In this context, economic factors gained weight: tuition fees became the second most relevant criterion when choosing a destination, just behind the prestige of the institutions, while scholarships and job prospects assume greater importance. According to New Oriental, the average family budget exceeds 600 thousand yuan.
The profile of students is also changing: 77% of demand is concentrated in higher education, with emphasis on undergraduate courses, and engineering continues to lead preferences, followed by economics. More than half of applicants choose to apply to multiple countries to reduce risks associated with changes in education policies. Despite these changes, the final objective remains: 88% of students plan to return to China after completing their training. Since the People's Republic of China sent the first group of students to the United States in 1979, the North American country has been one of the most prestigious destinations for Chinese students. In the 2023/2024 academic year, the 277,398 Chinese students enrolled represented 24.6% of the total 1.12 million international students in the United States. Lusa

item.title: Young people in Macau turn to AI for psychological advice
item.description: The Sheng Kung Hui Macau Social Services Coordinating Office carried out a survey among young people aged 13 to 35 on the use of the internet and artificial intelligence (AI) tools, concluding that approximately 39% of young people experience high levels of loneliness, indicating unmet social needs. It appears that almost 23% of young people choose to chat with AI when they feel “negative emotions”, with around 35% considering it “useful”. The results of the survey, cited by the newspaper Ou Mun, say that “the role of AI in emotional support should be viewed with caution”, encouraging young people to seek help from social workers, therapists and other professionals. Furthermore, the survey also shows that approximately 68% of respondents demonstrate a lack of understanding about “AI illusions”, for example, erroneous or misleading results, indicating an “insufficient awareness of the risks associated with AI applications”. Studies indicate a positive correlation between digital literacy and AI literacy, with higher levels of literacy correlating with lower levels of anxiety and depression, highlighting the importance of improving mental health literacy. A percentage of 65% of respondents have sleep disorders. Although almost 90% agree that physical exercise would be beneficial, only 60% exercise weekly, with an average duration of approximately 3.06 hours. The results of this survey show that the daily time spent using social media by young people has increased significantly, from approximately 1.64 hours in 2022 to approximately 3.53 hours in 2025. According to the Sheng Kung Hui association, although young people's self-assessment of digital literacy has improved, their practical scores in the fields of use and management and online ethics and awareness are relatively low, “reflecting the need for improvement in self-management and ethical judgment”.

item.title: Number of visitors has already exceeded 10 million
item.description:
From the beginning of the year until yesterday, Macau has already received 10 million visitors. The mark was reached 12 days earlier than last year. The daily average number of tourists is now 126,000, representing an increase of 14.2% compared to the previous year.
Yesterday morning, the milestone of 10 million visitors since the beginning of the year was reached. The bar was reached 12 days earlier last year, indicated a statement from the Public Security Police Corps (CPSP). The daily average number of tourists is now 126,000, representing an increase of 14.2% compared to the previous year. In demographic terms, tourists from mainland China represented the largest share, with 75.2%, tourists from Hong Kong represented 15.7%, 2.5% came from Taiwan and 6.6% were international tourists. As for the preferred border posts, Portas do Cerco was the most used, representing 44.1%, followed by the Hong Kong-Zhuhai-Macau Bridge border post, and then the Hengqin post, with 19.9% ​​and 16.1%, respectively. Quoted by Radio Macau in Chinese, Helena de Senna Fernandes, director of Tourism Services (DST), stated that, this year, the focus will continue to be the international tourist market. The official added that DST will explore the Central Asian market, inviting local travel agents to visit Macau, in order to better explore cooperation opportunities, with the aim of opening this new market as quickly as possible. Regarding the European market, Helena de Senna Fernandes stated that, this year, DST will launch online learning resources in English and German for travel agencies. DST has already discussed future cooperation with the German Association of Travel Agencies and will look to cooperate with other European travel agency associations in the future. DST will also strengthen cooperation with cultural centers and tourism offices of the Ministry of Culture and Tourism in Europe, according to the official. The Statistics and Census Bureau (DSEC) also reported on Friday that Macau received 4.17 million visitors in February, 32.6% more than in the same month of 2025, driven by the Lunar New Year holidays. The overwhelming majority of visitors to Macau in February came from mainland China, with 3.29 million arrivals, 43.6% more than in the same month last year. Hong Kong contributed 591,300 visitors (+2%) and Taiwan 89,628 (+31.9%). The number of international visitors fell 3.3%, to 200,848. In Southeast Asia, the Philippines stood out with 45,195 entries (+16.3%), while Indonesia (11,587) and Thailand (9,973) recorded drops of 22.3% and 11.4%, respectively. India had 5,831 visitors (+42.5%), while South Korea had 55,994 (-5.6%). Among long-distance markets, the United States of America registered 11,142 visitors (+5.5%). Attracting more foreign tourists to the territory has been one of the objectives repeatedly highlighted by Macau's tourism authorities. The territory's authorities set a target for 2025 to receive more than three million international tourists, however this figure remained at 2.76 million visitors, still an increase of 13.7% compared to 2024.

item.title: Lunar New Year pushes Macau to highest inflation in two years
item.description: Annual inflation in Macau reached the highest value in two years in February, due to the Lunar New Year, announced the Statistics and Census Bureau (DSEC). According to official data, the consumer price index (IPC) rose 1.16% compared to the same month last year, the highest value since February 2024. Inflation more than doubled compared to January (0.54%), something that DSEC justified with the Lunar New Year period taking place in February, unlike 2025, which took place in January. DSEC highlighted that leisure, recreation, sport and culture products and services recorded the biggest increase in annual terms (+7.76%), due to the rise in prices for hotel rooms and excursions (+26.6%). The region ended 2025 with annual inflation of 0.33%, the lowest value in the last four years. In June 2021, Macau experienced the last of 10 consecutive months of falling prices – or deflation – at the height of the economic crisis caused by the covid-19 pandemic. Deflation reflects weakness in domestic consumption and investment and is particularly serious, as a fall in the price of assets, normally contracted using credit, generates an imbalance between the value of loans and bank guarantees. Official data show that in February inflation was also felt in food products and non-alcoholic drinks (+0.92%). The cost of meals purchased outside the home rose 1.23%. Spending on apartment rent or mortgages rose 0.63% and 0.48%, respectively. On November 11, the Macau Monetary Authority approved the third interest rate cut this year. In April 2024, the territory's Legislative Assembly ended several taxes on the acquisition of housing, to “increase liquidity” in the real estate market, defended the Secretary for Economy and Finance, Lei Wai Nong, at the time. Due to the rise in the value of gold worldwide, the region recorded a 40.7% increase in the price of jewelry, goldsmithing and watches, products popular among tourists from mainland China. With the price of oil also soaring in international markets, the cost of plane tickets rose by almost a third (32.7%). In mainland China, by far Macau's largest trading partner, the CPI increased 1.3% in February, the biggest increase since January 2023. In the neighboring Hong Kong region, inflation also accelerated in February, to 1.7%, 0.6 percentage points more than in January.

item.title: Residents spend, on average, four hours a day on the internet
item.description:
Last year, the number of internet users in Macau was around 612.5 thousand, that is, 94% of the territory's total population. In 2021, the percentage was 88.5%, an increase of 5.5 percentage points in four years. The data was released by the Statistics and Census Bureau (DSEC), also revealing that, on average, Macau residents spend four hours a day on the internet.
The Statistics and Census Services Directorate (DSEC) released on Friday, on its online page, the results of the survey on the use of computer technology by Macao SAR households for last year. The results show that, on average, Macau residents spend around four hours a day on the internet. In the age group from 15 to 24 and also in the age group from 25 to 34, the average daily hours on the internet rises to five. Between 35 and 44 years old, the average is four hours and between 45 and 54 it drops to 3.5 hours per day of internet use. From 55 to 64 and also from 65 to 74, the average drops to three hours a day and in the age group over 75 the average is two hours. DSEC statistics also show that the number of individuals accessing the internet is growing. The number of internet users in Macau is 612.5 thousand, which represents 94% of the region's total population. Compared to 2024, the percentage remained identical, but four years ago, the internet penetration rate in Macau was 88.5%, thus registering an increase of 5.5 percentage points. The percentage of mobile phone users is now 94.3% and that of personal computer users is 61.1%, with slight increases in both cases compared to previous years. DSEC data shows that the percentage of households using computer technology is now 97.6%. In 2021, this percentage was 94.6%. The survey results suggest that there is a direct proportionality between the internet penetration rate and the academic qualifications of users. The internet penetration rate of users who had academic qualifications corresponding to higher education was the highest (99.4%), followed by the rates of users who had academic qualifications corresponding to complementary secondary education (98.8%) and general secondary education (96.4%). The internet penetration rate among individuals with primary education or less is 81.6%. Another of the survey's conclusions is that the vast majority of people use the internet to communicate or access social networks. In second place comes entertainment and thirdly online banking services or mobile payments. The percentage of internet users who made online purchases also increased by 26.8% to a total of around 311 thousand. Online purchases of food and drink take-away services (173,500 people) and clothes, shoes, bags, accessories, etc. (170,100 people) were made by more users. In the fourth quarter of 2025, the median expenditure on online purchases amounted to 1,300 patacas.

item.title: International Forum brings together UNESCO representatives, content creators and academics
item.description: The International Gastronomy Forum, Macau, takes place today, between 9:30 am and 12:30 pm, as part of the International Festival of Gastronomy Cities, Macau 2026, organized by the Tourism Services Directorate (DST). The event, at Fisherman's Wharf, brings together representatives from the UNESCO Creative Cities Network, the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations, top international content creators and representatives from the restaurant and academic sectors will be speakers at the forum. The event will also be attended by representatives from nearly 50 member cities of the UNESCO Creative Cities Network in the area of ​​gastronomy and other creative areas, experts and academics, as well as the tourism and restaurant sector of Macau, “in a meeting to promote the transmission, innovation and exchange of Creative Cities of Gastronomy, with a view to creating a new intersectoral creative force and highlighting the advantages of Macau as a platform for international exchange and cooperation”, explains DST. This forum features three thematic lectures and three parallel thematic discussions, under the theme “Creative Fusion: Reimagining Gastronomy Beyond the Plate”. “A group of influential speakers will explore how intersectoral cooperation in the area of ​​gastronomy can boost the development of a creative city, combining stories, experiences and innovation, becoming a new force that connects everyday life and sustainable development”, adds DST. The International Festival of Cities of Gastronomy, Macau 2026 ends on March 29, at Praça do Coliseu Romano and Legend Boulevard at Macau Fisherman's Wharf. A new event location was also created this year, located on Rua de Cantão and Rua de Shanghai.

item.title: Macau requires “radical measures” in the face of the lowest birth rate in the world
item.description:
Researchers told Lusa that, to reverse the lowest birth rate in the world, Macau needs “more radical measures” than mere subsidies to improve the lives of the entire population.
In 2025, the region registered 2,871 newborns, the lowest number in almost half a century. In the previous year, the fertility rate had been 0.58 births per woman, far from what was needed to replace generations (2.1). According to estimates by the United Nations Department of Economic and Social Affairs, Macau will have the lowest birth rate in the world in 2024, followed by Singapore, with a fertility rate of 0.95 births per woman. Something that “reflects structural pressures common in many urban economies in East Asia where the cost of living is high”, said Emma Zang Xiaolu, a sociologist at Yale University, in the United States. In Hong Kong, 15 primary schools are at risk of closing after the neighboring Chinese region recorded around 31,100 births in 2025, the lowest number ever. Mainland China recorded 7.92 million births in 2025, the lowest figure since the founding of the People's Republic of China in 1949. The birth rate has also fallen to historic lows. “Those very conventional life trajectories, about the age at which you should get married and, after you get married, you should have children, those norms have been weakening,” said Mu Zheng, a sociologist at the National University of Singapore. As East Asia has become more developed and wealthier, people "value individual preferences more," although the influence of "family and family values" is even greater than in the West, he added. But family pressure collides with “many other types of pressures”, Mu Zheng stressed, including expectations of good academic performance and, later, a “very demanding” work culture. Emma Zang also points to “long working hours” as factors that make it difficult to start a family, along with “housing costs and uncertainty about future economic opportunities”. Macau was the 13th most expensive city in the world in 2024, according to a survey by the North American consultancy company Mercer, mainly due to housing prices. The pressures felt in other East Asian cities “are intensified” in Macau, highlighted Emma Zang, “by a highly specialized economy and limited housing space”. Having children is not yet in Emily Cheong's plans, 29 years old, despite having been married for three years. “We are still living with my parents, trying to save for a down payment on an apartment”, explained the resident to Lusa. The couple's situation became complicated last year, when the 'satellite casino' where Cheong's husband worked, a 'croupier' who managed to find a new job, but with a lower salary, closed. Ten ‘satellite casinos’ – spaces under the jurisdiction of gaming concessionaires, but managed by other companies – closed their doors in 2025, before the deadline imposed when the legislation regulating casinos was changed, in 2022. The Government introduced measures to encourage birth rates, such as a subsidy, worth a total of MOP54,000, for children up to three years old. But Emma Zang is not optimistic: “Government incentives may help marginally, but global studies suggest that financial subsidies alone rarely reverse the decline.” “Many of these policies are not particularly effective,” confirmed Mu Zheng, because the incentives “may not be large enough to really remove all the anxiety” from young adults. The sociologist gave as an example the expectation, placed on parents, of “an intensive investment of time, energy and money in the development and education of their children”. Emma Zang believes that “improved housing affordability, work-family balance, and childcare availability tend to be more important for long-term demographic stabilization.” The Macau Government promised to offer, free of charge, more and better daycare centers and launched a public consultation, which ended on Monday, on the increase, in the private sector, of maternity leave, from 70 to 90 days. Policies that “really support young families and encourage gender equality”, particularly in the division of domestic labor, can work, but Mu Zheng emphasizes that even in Scandinavia the effect has faded. The sociologist says that, in the long term, “more radical and comprehensive measures” are needed, including “creating a work culture that is more friendly” to family life and “reducing the pressure on mental health”. Lusa

item.title: Hengqin is “crucial task” for cooperation between Guangdong and Macau, considers Sam Hou Fai
item.description:
The Chief Executive was present at the Guangdong Spring Dinner and, in his speech, highlighted the importance of cooperation between Macau and the neighboring province, whose “crucial task” is the Hengqin project. Sam Hou Fai assured that the Macao SAR will align with national development strategies and “take advantage of the momentum to deepen and consolidate cooperation between Guangdong and Macau, and provide better service to the national development situation”.
Sam Hou Fai participated in the Guangdong Province Spring Dinner and took the opportunity to highlight the importance of the Macao SAR's cooperation with the neighboring province. In his speech on the occasion, the Chief Executive began by noting that the 15th national Five-Year Plan will allow for a “broader space in which it becomes possible to deepen cooperation between Guangdong and Macau”. Therefore, Sam promised that “the MSAR will work together with the province of Guangdong to study, fulfill and carry forward the spirit of the series of important speeches delivered by President Xi Jinping” and, on the other hand, he also guaranteed that the provisions of the 15th Five-Year Plan relating to Guangdong and Macau will be “consciously” implemented. "We will ensure effective coordination between the 3rd Five-Year Plan of the Macao SAR and the 15th Five-Year Plan of Guangdong, in order to elevate cooperation between Guangdong and Macau to new heights and new levels. Together, we will contribute to opening new horizons in the construction of a strong country with Chinese-style modernization and the great rejuvenation of the Chinese nation", he stressed. In his speech, the Macao SAR Chief Executive praised the evolution of China's economy over the past few years, expressing himself “very pleased” to note that, until last year, “the Guangdong-Hong Kong-Macao Greater Bay Area reached a new level in economic production, accelerating its development to become a world-class bay”. “Over the past year, under the guidance and support of the central authorities and through the joint efforts of Guangdong and Macau, cooperation between the two parties has produced fruitful results,” said the Chief Executive, highlighting the organization, for the first time, of the 15th edition of the National Games and the Special Olympic Games for the Disabled by Guangdong, Hong Kong and Macau, as well as the holding of the Joint Cooperation Conference Guangdong-Macao-2025. Sam argued that “the two-way flow of people between Guangdong and Macau has become increasingly dynamic”, noting that, last year, Macau received approximately 40.07 million visitors, setting a new record. Among these, visitors from Guangdong province totaled around 17.1 million, representing 42.7% of the total and an increase of 30.4% compared to the previous year. Since the implementation of the Macau Vehicle Circulation policy in Guangdong Province, the number of crossings of vehicles with unique Macau registration plates through the Hong Kong-Zhuhai-Macao Bridge Border Post reached 4.39 million, with 1.74 million registered in 2025 alone – an increase of 14.1% compared to the previous year. Likewise, the Cooperation Zone in Hengqin has achieved new results in phases in its development. In 2025, the region's regional GDP reached RMB 54.7 billion, with total retail sales of consumer goods worth RMB 5.7 billion, representing an annual increase of 20.3%. The number of business entities with capital from Macau reached 7,800, including 7,681 companies, significantly strengthening the level of integration between Macau and Hengqin. “New progress was also achieved in the development of the Guangdong-Hong Kong-Macao Greater Bay Area,” he said. This year also marks the preparation of the Macao SAR's 3rd Five-Year Plan. According to the Chief Executive, the document must take into account and “exhaustively” implement the “spirit enshrined in the series of important speeches given by President Xi Jinping, comprehensively, precisely and unshakably apply the principles of ‘one country, two systems’ and ‘Macao governed by its people’ with a high degree of autonomy”. “We will proactively align ourselves with national development strategies, take advantage of opportunities and seize the momentum to deepen and consolidate cooperation between Guangdong and Macau, and provide better service to the national development situation”, he stressed. In this context, the leader of the Macao SAR Government considers that “the high-quality development of the Hengqin Cooperation Zone constitutes the crucial task for cooperation between Guangdong and Macau in the new circumstances”. Sam Hou Fai said that he and the Governor of the neighboring province, Meng Fanli, have “an inalienable responsibility to strengthen cooperation, be united and lead all sectors of Guangdong and Macau in implementing the spirit enshrined in the important guidelines issued by President Xi Jinping regarding the development of Hengqin”. “We remain fully aware that the original intention of exploring Hengqin is to fulfill the fundamental mission of promoting the adequate diversification of Macau’s economy”, reinforced the Chief Executive, adding: “We must consistently adhere to the strategic positioning of ‘Macau + Hengqin’, align ourselves with the reference criteria and operational requirements of the ‘three checks’ and ‘three connectivities’, jointly implement the strategic guidelines of the Central Government, firmly reform, pool efforts and overcome challenges, to open a new juncture in the development of the Hengqin Cooperation Zone.” Sam Hou Fai said he hopes that Guangdong Province will “continue to firmly support cooperation between Macau and Hengqin, further increase investment in the Hengqin Cooperation Zone, effectively implement measures that channel high-quality projects from Guangdong to establish themselves in the Zone, focus on priority sectors, so that distinctive industries with their own characteristics are developed, enhancing the synergistic effect of industry clustering and, through the creation of a portfolio of emblematic enterprises and projects with a significant multiplier effect, a new and greater impetus will be injected into the integrated development of Macau and Hengqin, as well as the integration of the Guangdong-Hong Kong-Macao Greater Bay Area market”.

item.title: China's restriction on fuel and fertilizer exports raises fears
item.description:
China is restricting exports of refined fuels and fertilizers, a decision that is increasing fears of shortages in countries dependent on these products, including Brazil, given the instability caused by the war in the Middle East.
According to the British newspaper Financial Times, the National Development and Reform Commission (NDRC), China's main economic planning body, recently ordered fertilizer producers to suspend shipments abroad. The move follows previous instructions to large state-owned refineries to halt exports of jet fuel, diesel and kerosene. Although Beijing has not officially announced export controls, industry sources, diplomats and analysts confirmed the adoption of restrictions, according to the newspaper. China is the world's second-largest exporter of fertilizers, behind Russia, and the sixth-largest exporter of jet fuel. Brazil is the main destination for Chinese exports of nitrogen fertilizers, phosphate fertilizers and compound fertilizers. The restrictions are already having an impact in other countries. India, which sources around 10% of its fertilizer imports from China, could face constraints. The Philippines is looking for alternative suppliers, while Vietnam, heavily dependent on aviation fuel imports, warns of possible disruptions from April onwards. In Australia, the situation is also getting worse. The country depends on China for around a third of its jet fuel and is one of the main importers of Chinese diesel. The Australian Government has already warned of the risk of rising inflation and called an emergency meeting to respond to the crisis. Analysts indicate that the objective of the Chinese authorities is to preserve energy and agricultural reserves and protect the internal market, at a time when instability in the Middle East, namely the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, limits access to essential raw materials, such as sulfur. Experts consider it likely that restrictions will be extended. “There is a full effort to ensure that Chinese farmers have sufficient access to fertilizers before any exports,” said Even Pay, from consultancy Trivium China, cited by the FT. The lack of a formal announcement reflects, according to analysts, the speed with which the measures were implemented. Consultant S&P Global Energy considers it unlikely to grant exceptions for fuel exports while disruptions to navigation in the Persian Gulf persist. Lusa

item.title: Portuguese cork becomes art in the hands of Chinese Jinky Huang
item.description:
Portuguese cork has gained new life in the sculptures and installations of contemporary Chinese artist Jinky Huang, who has been using the material imported from Portugal for two decades to promote a vision of sustainable art.
Huang told Lusa that he discovered cork around 20 years ago, when he lived in Italy, where he spent part of his childhood and youth. “I saw some materials made from cork that were for sale and I thought it was a very special material”, he recalled. The variety of applications was limited at the time, but the creative potential aroused his immediate interest. "I like working with new and special materials. I was already making installations, sculptures and even clothes with different materials. When I discovered cork I realized that it could have many more shapes and possibilities", he said. The artist claims to have developed hundreds of techniques and applications for the material, which he now uses in sculptures, furniture and design objects, including chairs, sculptures and accessories. “From the point of view of sustainability, recycling and respect for the environment, cork is one of the best materials in the world”, he said. The raw material used in his works is imported from Portugal, a country that the artist has already visited to contact producers. “Portugal has the best cork,” said Huang, adding that the thickness and resistance of Portuguese cork are superior to that produced in China. In 2025, Portuguese cork exports reached around 148 thousand tons, worth 1.1 billion euros, according to data from the National Statistics Institute (INE), an increase of 1.7% compared to 2024. Despite working with cork for around 20 years, Huang recognized that this material remains relatively little known in the Asian country. “Most people in China only come into contact with cork through wine corks,” he said. According to Huang, his career helped to promote cork in the country: “Today, when many people in China talk about cork art, they think of me”. In 2024, Portugal exported 19 million euros worth of cork and cork products to China, according to international trade data based on UN Comtrade. Although China is a relevant market in Asia, the United States and European countries continue to absorb a large part of Portuguese cork. Over time, the use of cork led Huang to delve deeper into the concept of sustainability in artistic creation. "At first I didn't think so much about sustainability. I just wanted to do something different. It was cork that made me really understand what sustainability, recycling and respect for the environment mean," he said. The focus on this material was recently at the center of the exhibition “Deconstruct the Past Codes Hero: the Sustainable Roads”, presented in Shanghai, the economic “capital” of China, where Huang resides and maintains his studio. The exhibition, which received tens of thousands of visitors, reinterpreted two iconic figures from Asian popular culture – the Monkey King, from the Chinese mythological novel Journey to the West, and Astro Boy, a character created by Japanese Osamu Tezuka – through sculptures and installations inspired by sustainability. Among the highlights was a sculpture of the Monkey King made from cork. In recent years, the artist has presented exhibitions in several Chinese cities and says that interest in his work has increased. “More and more people are getting to know my work and visiting the exhibitions,” he said. In 2025, he held four exhibitions in China and is now preparing new international exhibitions. According to Huang, exhibitions are confirmed in Singapore, Thailand and New York, while negotiations are underway for new projects in Europe. In addition to artistic activity, Huang teaches and lectures at Chinese universities, such as the Central Academy of Fine Arts in Beijing, the Academy of Fine Arts of China and Tongji University in Shanghai. "My goal is for more artists to join this idea. I want new generations to participate in this movement", he stated.

item.title: Philippines authorizes use of more polluting fuel in transport and industry
item.description: The Philippines has temporarily and partially authorized the use of more polluting Euro II standard oil products in the transport and industrial sectors, as a contingency measure due to the energy supply crisis, it was announced yesterday. The Department of Energy (DOE) of this Asian country, which relies heavily on imports from the Middle East, said in a statement that this is a “timely response to external risks affecting specific sectors”, without specifying how long the measure should remain in force. The measure allows the provisional use of Euro II fuels in vehicles manufactured until 2015 and in 'jeepneys' - the popular Philippine buses -, as well as in the maritime and transport sector and in industrial applications, such as power plants and generators. According to the statement, those who intend to use or offer this type of fuel must notify the Philippine Petroleum Industry Management Bureau (OIMB) and establishments selling it must post “clear and visible notices” explicitly indicating that the fuel is Euro II. Furthermore, companies that offer this type of fuel must keep it separate from Euro IV, the standard commonly used in the archipelago, in storage, transport and sales systems. Euro II and Euro IV standards, among others, are used in European Union (EU) legislation on vehicle emissions and serve as an international reference in countries such as the Philippines. The objective of the measure adopted by the archipelago, motivated by consultations with the oil and automobile industries, is to “maintain a continuous supply of fuel”, while offering some “flexibility to sectors that may be affected” by the energy crisis resulting from the conflict in the Middle East. The Philippines, which, along with other Southeast Asian countries such as Thailand, has been facing long queues at gas stations due to rising fuel prices, two weeks ago reduced the work week to four days in several government departments. The power station in the island city of Patnanungan, in the northeast of the country, reduced its operating hours by eight hours from this Sunday and for an indefinite period due to fuel shortages.

item.title: North Korean leader and his daughter watch a military exercise with anti-missile equipment
item.description:
The North Korean leader, accompanied by his daughter, supervised offensive military exercises during which a new tank capable of intercepting drone and missile attacks was tested. Several tests were carried out on the “active protection system” of a new main battle tank, capable of intercepting anti-tank missiles and drones “with 100% effectiveness”.
North Korean leader Kim Jong-un, accompanied by his daughter Kim Ju-ae, supervised offensive military exercises during which a new tank capable of intercepting drone and missile attacks was tested, the KCNA news agency reported. Kim observed a joint tactical attack exercise with infantry troops and tank units on Thursday, the same day that joint US-South Korean maneuvers ended at a base in Pyongyang that featured drones and armored vehicles. As the simulation began, several attack drones struck enemy positions based on real-time reconnaissance data, while other attack units in the rear destroyed enemy drones. Subsequently, these units invaded and occupied the enemy's defensive lines and fire positions. Several tests were carried out on the “active protection system” of a new main battle tank, capable of intercepting anti-tank missiles and drones “with 100% effectiveness”, reported the North Korean state news agency. According to Kim, this equipment has no parallel “in the world” in terms of firepower, mobility and defensive capabilities. Images of the exercises show the North Korean leader and his daughter — who is speculated to be Kim's possible successor — aboard a tank during the visit to the base where the maneuvers took place, in which a company from the cavalry regiment, the main armored unit of the reserve operations group and special operations units participated. North Korea's military exercises took place in parallel with the last day of the annual joint Freedom Shield maneuvers between the United States and South Korea, considered by Pyongyang as invasion rehearsals against its territory. The Washington and Seoul maneuvers, in which around 18,000 military personnel participated, began on March 9 and took place amid speculation about a possible transfer of North American Patriot anti-missile systems from South Korea to the Middle East, in the context of the US and Israeli war against Iran. Just one day after the start of these joint exercises, Kim Yo-jong, the influential sister of the North Korean leader, warned that they could lead to “terrible consequences”. KCNA reported that Kim Jong-un supervised, on March 10, a test launch of cruise missiles from a destroyer and that, four days later, the regime carried out a test of “twelve ultra-precise multiple launchers of 600 millimeters in caliber”. Lusa

item.title: Michelin Guide 2026 distinguishes 21 restaurants in Macau with stars
item.description:
The 18th edition of the Michelin Guide Hong Kong and Macau was unveiled on March 19 in Macau. The 2026 selection, which marks the centenary of the awarding of Michelin stars globally, distinguishes a total of 278 restaurants in the two territories, of which 59 in Macau. In total, 21 restaurants in Macau received one, two or three stars, with emphasis on the maintenance of the three-star status of Jade Dragon and Robuchon au Dôme, and the entry of two new establishments into the one-star category.
The ceremony revealing the Michelin Guide Hong Kong and Macau 2026 brought good news for Macau's gastronomy, which saw its number of starred restaurants increase to 21. This year's edition, which celebrates the centenary of the creation of the Michelin star system, once again highlighted the territory's culinary excellence, to once again expand its status as a UNESCO Creative City of Gastronomy. At the top of the list, Macau has two restaurants with the maximum distinction of three Michelin stars. Jade Dragon, in the City of Dreams, secured its top position for the eighth consecutive year, consolidating itself as one of the most acclaimed Cantonese cuisine establishments in the region. Robuchon au Dôme, at Grand Lisboa, was also awarded three stars, maintaining its excellence in the region's contemporary French cuisine. In the two-star category, Macau has six restaurants. Highlights include Alain Ducasse at Morpheus, in the City of Dreams, which maintains its distinction for the eighth consecutive year, and The Huaiyang Garden, specializing in Huaiyang cuisine, which is also part of this exclusive group. The list also includes Chef Tam’s Seasons, Feng Wei Ju (Hunan and Sichuan cuisine), The Eight and Wing Lei, all of which are recognized for the consistency and quality of their offerings. The one Michelin star category in Macau now has 13 restaurants, after the entry of two new establishments. Don Alfonso 1890, an Italian restaurant in the Grand Lisboa Palace, was promoted to the one-star category, as was the Palace Garden, also in the same hotel complex, which stands out for its Cantonese cuisine. These two new award winners join a list that includes names such as 8½ Otto and Mezzo – Bombana (which also has a presence in Hong Kong), Aji, Five Foot Road (specializing in Sichuan cuisine), Lai Heen, Mizumi (Japanese cuisine), Pearl Dragon (Cantonese and Chaozhou cuisine, in Studio City), Sushi Kinetsu and Sushi Kissho by Miyakawa (both specializing in sushi), Ying ( Cantonese at Altira Macau), Zi Yat Heen and Zuicho (Japanese cuisine). Macau's performance in this edition of the Michelin Guide presents the result of consistent work by hotel operators and local kitchen teams in recent years, as the territory continues its saga of establishing itself as a reference gastronomic destination throughout Asia. The presence of 21 starred restaurants in Macau, in a small territory, places the city among the destinations with the highest density of Michelin stars in the world, alongside other gastronomic capitals such as Tokyo, Paris or Singapore, statistically. The recognition covers a diverse range of cuisines that particularly highlights the plurality of high-level local gastronomic offerings. In addition to the stars, the Michelin Guide also recognized the commitment to sustainability through the award of the Michelin Green Star. In Macau, this award was given to the Educational Restaurant of the University of Tourism of Macau (UTM Educational Restaurant), which stands out for its practices that combine culinary excellence with environmental responsibility. The award ceremony, which took place at the Grand Lisboa Palace Resort Macau, also included the awarding of special prizes to professionals in the field, although this year's winners were mainly from Hong Kong. The new “Mentor Chef Award” category was awarded to chef Lau Ping Lui, from Tin Lung Heen, in Hong Kong. Overall, the Michelin Guide Hong Kong and Macau 2026 selection includes 278 establishments, of which 59 are in Macau. The increase in the number of starred restaurants in Macau, which increased from 19 to 21 compared to the previous year, also demonstrates the result of the continued commitment to gastronomic excellence as one of the pillars of its strategy of economic diversification and attraction of quality tourism beyond gambling.

item.title: National security: Government determined in reinforced work in the cultural area
item.description:
The Macau Government will further strengthen the supervision of national security in the cultural and economic area with the entry into operation of the new State Security Defense Commission (CDSE). Wong Sio Chak, secretary for Administration and Justice, said that education and culture authorities will have to take on “considerable work” in defending national security, under instructions from the CDSE. The Executive will also create a CDSE Secretariat with up to five departments, and the identity of the majority of employees will not be disclosed to the public.
Education and cultural authorities will be obliged to reinforce their work on raising awareness and safeguarding national security, carrying it out in accordance with the instructions of the new Commission for the Defense of State Security (CDSE) Wong Sio Chak, Secretary for Administration and Justice, highlighted last Friday the importance of national security in the cultural area, particularly in the protection of traditional Chinese culture. “To destroy a country, especially in modern times, it is not only necessary to use military or online means, but it is possible to resort to a process of cultural intervention or elimination, as well as cultural nihilism, leading the country to lose knowledge of its own culture”, said Wong Sio Chak. He also added that it is “extremely important” to promote traditional culture to avoid “damage caused to the survival of the country or nation”. It is recalled that the Legislative Assembly approved last week a new law that expands the competence of the State Security Defense Commission, a body that was created in 2018 to support the Government leader in making decisions relating to China's national security. After this new legislation comes into force, the Commission will also include, as members, the directors of the Directorate of Gaming Inspection and Coordination (DICJ), the Cultural Institute (IC) and the Directorate of Education and Youth Development Services (DSEDJ). Wong Sio Chak pointed out that the IC and DSEDJ will be responsible for national security work in the cultural area, while the DICJ will work for state security in the economic sphere, and these bodies “will have to take on a considerable amount” of safeguarding national security in the future. The secretary reiterated, in this case, that the implementation of the “General National Security Perspective” in the region is not simply related to traditional military areas, territories and sovereignty, but also to “non-traditional” aspects. Thus, the Chief Executive will have the power, as necessary, to summon any directors of departments or specialists from areas not included in the composition of the Commission to participate in meetings and work. The Macau Executive's program for 2026 foresees the creation, by the end of the year, of a Working Group for the Coordination of Patriotic Education for Young People. The Lines of Government Action also include, within the scope of the so-called “general plan for education on national security”, the review of books and other teaching materials for primary and secondary education, until the third quarter of 2027. In 2022, the then Secretary for Security of Macau, Wong Sio Chak, also said that education on State security would be extended to non-Chinese students.
SECRETARIAT WITH “CONFIDENTIAL” STAFF STATUS
At Friday's Executive Council press conference, Wong Sio Chak, also the body's spokesperson, presented the administrative regulations for the CDSE Secretariat, as a permanent execution and support service for the aforementioned Commission. According to him, the Secretariat's staff is made up of 34 people, including the secretary general, a role carried out simultaneously by the secretary for Security; two deputy secretaries-general and two deputy secretaries-general, appointed by the Chief Executive, the list of which will be disclosed to the public. The body will have five departments: the Policy Study Department, the Risk Prevention and Control Department, the Liaison and Coordination Department, the Publicity and Education Department and the General Support Department, with five directors and 24 employees, whose status “is confidential under the law”, according to Wong Sio Chak, so his identity is kept confidential and will not be disclosed. "The selection [of personnel] will be conducted in an extremely rigorous manner. Appointments will be made in accordance with the forms provided for in general legislation and special legislation, including secondments, transfers, administrative provision contracts and individual employment contracts. The project presented by the Executive Council also states that the CDSE Secretariat reports to the president of the CDSE, who is the Chief Executive, and operates, in the administrative structure, directly dependent on the Chief Executive, and enjoys administrative autonomy. On the other hand, the current law on the organization and organic structure of public services and entities determines that departments with a staff of less than 200 people must have up to two subunits at department level and four subunits at division level, and the present Secretariat exceeds these general provisions. Wong Sio Chak, in response, also highlighted that the creation of this Secretariat is a “special” department that the Chief Executive decides on the basis of “specific needs”, thus being able to follow the special provisions of the regime. The Secretariat's regulations will be published on the same day as the CDSE law and will come into force on the day following its publication.
